vulnerability.title: Disable Kernel Parameter for IPv4 Forwarding on all IPv4 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matching_list" fi done # # Set sysctl

triage_decision.reason: The change modifies a kernel sysctl parameter that controls IPv4 forwarding; it is a low‑risk networking setting that can be safely disabled without affecting authentication or boot processes.

# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation sets net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding to 0 but relies on a fixed line number and fragile sed patterns, which may miss some entries or cause syntax issues.
concerns: Hardcoded line number edit in /etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf may break if file content changes
Escaping and regex in sed commands could be incorrect, potentially corrupting the file
Does not verify that the sysctl parameter is truly set to 0 after applying changes
May not cover all locations where the parameter could be defined
suggested_improvements: Search and replace the exact line matching net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding with a robust pattern, rather than editing line 72
Iterate over all candidate sysctl files and comment out or set the parameter to 0 wherever it appears
Validate the resulting sysctl value with 'sysctl -p' or 'sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.forwarding' to ensure it is 0
Create backups of modified files before making changes
